message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

content.text:
Execute git commit with these ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS:

CONSTRAINTS:
1. NO Claude/Anthropic/AI attribution in commit message
2. PRIORITY: If project's CLAUDE.md exists and specifies commit style, use that style FIRST
   - Always check for project-specific commit conventions in CLAUDE.md
   - Project's commit style takes precedence over default Conventional Commits

COMMIT MESSAGE FORMAT:
- Default Pattern: type(scope): description
- Types: feat, fix, refactor, chore, docs, style, test, remove
- Language: English
- Examples:
  - feat(search): add new search feature
  - fix(cart): resolve checkout error
  - docs(readme): update installation guide

AUTO MODE:
- If `--auto` flag is present in <additional_instructions>, enable auto mode
- In auto mode: skip user selection (step 6), automatically choose the best commit message and execute immediately
- Strip `--auto` from the additional instructions before using them as supplementary context

GIT FLAGS PASSTHROUGH:
- If `--no-gpg-sign` flag is present in <additional_instructions>, append `--no-gpg-sign` to the git commit command
- If `--no-verify` flag is present in <additional_instructions>, append `--no-verify` to the git commit command
- Strip these flags from the additional instructions before using them as supplementary context

PROCESS:
1. Check: Look for CLAUDE.md in project root for commit style specifications
2. Check: git status
3. Verify: only "Changes to be committed" will be included
4. Review: git log to understand project's commit style
5. Generate: Create 4 different commit message options based on the changes
6. Present (skip in auto mode): Use AskUserQuestion to show only the commit heading (first line) for each option
7. Commit: Execute git commit with the selected message (full message with body if applicable)

COMMIT MESSAGE OPTIONS:
- Generate 4 different commit messages with varying:
  - Commit types (feat, fix, refactor, chore, docs, style, etc.)
  - Scope specificity (broader vs. narrower scope)
  - Description detail level (concise vs. detailed)
- Each option should accurately reflect the staged changes
- Only display the heading (first line, e.g., "feat(auth): add JWT token validation") in AskUserQuestion
- Keep the full commit message (with body if needed) ready for execution after selection

AUTO-SELECTION CRITERIA (when --auto is active):
- Choose the most accurate commit type reflecting the primary change
- Use specific scope when changes are focused on one area
- Use broader scope or omit when changes span multiple areas
- Prefer concise, descriptive messages
- Include body only for complex changes requiring explanation

Violation of these constraints should result in immediate task termination.

<additional_instructions>

</additional_instructions>

content.content:
4f8ae8b Merge branch 'feat/tmux-pane'
1f343cb feat(pane): use Codex nickname for pane title and drop agentId prefix
c1244b6 refactor(codex): simplify retry config, ID mapping, and subagent prefill
4ac0215 fix(codex): fix pane lifecycle and output filter bugs in --pane mode
17cb370 chore(claude): add format-on-edit hook and Claude settings
5993566 fix(codex): increase subagent file retry to 30×300ms (9s total)
8c52dc6 fix(codex): filter subagent output when pane is open
be6b854 docs(claude.md): update for Codex Phase 2+3 completion
efd88c3 docs: mark Phase 2+3 complete in codex subagent support spec
e34a05f fix(simplify): correct step numbering in startCodexMultiWatch (10/11 order)

+        "matcher": "Task",

+            "command": "entire hooks claude-code post-todo"
+          }
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "PreToolUse": [
+      {
+        "matcher": "Task",
+        "hooks": [
+          {
+            "type": "command",
+            "command": "entire hooks claude-code pre-task"

+        "hooks": [
+          {

+            "command": "entire hooks claude-code session-end"

+    "SessionStart": [
+      {
+        "matcher": "",
+        "hooks": [
+          {
+            "type": "command",
+            "command": "entire hooks claude-code session-start"
+          }
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "Stop": [
+      {
+        "matcher": "",
+        "hooks": [
+          {
+            "type": "command",
+            "command": "entire hooks claude-code stop"
+          }
+        ]
+      }
+    ],
+    "UserPromptSubmit": [
+      {
+        "matcher": "",
+        "hooks": [
+          {
+            "type": "command",
+            "command": "entire hooks claude-code user-prompt-submit"
           }
         ]
       }
     ]
+  },
+  "permissions": {
+    "deny": [
+      "Read(./.entire/metadata/**)"
+    ]
   }
 }

content.text: Changes are: adding `entire` CLI integration (hooks for session tracking, git hooks) and updating Claude settings with `entire` hooks + permission deny rule.

options.label: chore: integrate entire CLI for session capture
options.description: broad chore type covering all entire-related additions

options.label: chore(entire): add entire CLI hooks and settings
feat(entire): integrate entire CLI session tracking
options.description: treats entire integration as a new feature

options.label: chore: add entire hooks, husky git hooks, and settings
options.description: more detailed description of what was added

options.description: broad chore type covering all entire-related additions

options.label: chore(entire): add entire CLI hooks and settings
feat(entire): integrate entire CLI session tracking
options.description: treats entire integration as a new feature

options.description: more detailed description of what was added

answers.選擇 commit message？: chore: integrate entire CLI for session capture

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore: integrate entire CLI for session capture

- Add entire hooks to Claude settings (session lifecycle, task, todo, prompt)
- Add husky git hooks for entire (commit-msg, post-commit, pre-push, prepare-commit-msg)
- Add .entire/ config with settings.json and .gitignore
- Add permission deny rule for .entire/metadata
EOF
)"

content.content: PreToolUse:Bash hook error: [uv run ~/.claude/hooks/pre_tool_use_light.py]: BLOCKED: Git command 'git hooks for entire (commit-msg, post-commit, pre-push, prepare-commit-msg)' is not allowed.

toolUseResult: Error: PreToolUse:Bash hook error: [uv run ~/.claude/hooks/pre_tool_use_light.py]: BLOCKED: Git command 'git hooks for entire (commit-msg, post-commit, pre-push, prepare-commit-msg)' is not allowed.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore: integrate entire CLI for session capture

- Add entire hooks to Claude settings (session lifecycle, task, todo, prompt)
- Add husky hooks for entire integration
- Add .entire/ config with settings.json and .gitignore
- Add permission deny rule for .entire/metadata
EOF
)"
input.description: Create commit with entire CLI integration
